How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-29 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing across 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, ranking validated combo matches above raw overlap count.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMAT is the standout at score 280 on CLENOW_MOMENTUM, CS_RANKER, and VOLUME_LEADER, the widest margin above the pack. ABSI (160) layers BREAKOUT, PULLBACK, and SHORT_SQUEEZE, a squeeze-into-breakout read with 3 screeners but no validated combo match. CAT (149) adds VARIANCE_PREMIUM to a similar momentum-pullback stack, making it the cleanest macro-adjacent name. The critical weakness in today's batch: not one ticker carries a matched_combo, meaning none hit SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 or the 3+ VALID screeners threshold that anchors the highest-confidence signals. Score distribution is top-heavy with AMAT as an outlier and the remaining nine clustered 94-160. NEUTRAL macro (54.11) is a reasonable backdrop for PULLBACK setups, which run through almost every name here, but without a validated combo the batch sits at medium conviction. AMAT first, on score alone, but size accordingly given the absence of any tier-1 combo confirmation.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: ALL (score 4, target ~$227.87 (DD20d -4.3%)).

3 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, filtered through EMA8/21 trend structure and low-OI anomaly scoring.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑60 Watch$87.45 +0.10%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—94STRONG88
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking has only 5 resolved trades against a 290-signal pipeline, well below the 20-trade minimum for a reliable verdict, so the reported 100% win rate and 200% average 20-day gain are statistically meaningless and cannot confirm or contradict the backtest's 68% win rate and 3.84% mean PnL - the key number here is 5, which is too thin to say anything.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 290 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is threadbare: one signal, TLT at score 60 in the Watch tier on a bullish sweep. Score distribution is trivially concentrated, with zero names clearing 65 and nothing near 80, so there is no graduation of conviction to work with. A long-duration bond play is defensible under a NEUTRAL macro regime (54.11) if the rate narrative is turning, but TLT at Watch-tier with a 60 score is a monitoring position, not a trade. If forced to act, TLT is the only name available, sized small and treated as a recon entry rather than a conviction add.

🧠 Ranks premium-selling candidates by IV30 minus HV30 spread, Sinclair sector adjustment, and value factors; edge is persistent overpricing of implied vol relative to realized.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener works: 63% win rate and +3.4% average per trade versus SPY at -0.9% is a 4.3-point live edge, and monthly cohorts trending from 53% in April to 62% in May are moving toward, not away from, the backtest Sharpe of 2.34.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG leads at 78 and is the cleanest name: a utility, which Sinclair's Table 4.7 flags with a +31% sector premium bias, making the IV30 overcharge more structurally reliable. KMB at 76 offers a similar low-realized-vol profile in staples, a natural premium-selling sector. D, ES, SO, and ED at 73-74 form a utility cluster that reinforces the sector tilt but adds concentration risk. The 44-signal batch is tightly clustered - median 67, max 78, zero names above 80 - so there are no high-conviction outliers and the range is narrow. NEUTRAL macro at 54 is not a tailwind but does not actively suppress vol premium, so selling into it is defensible. Start with PCG at moderate conviction, standard short-vega sizing, given top score and cleanest sector alignment.

🧠 Identifies stocks where implied vol sits in the MID VRP tertile, targeting options priced coherently with realized vol for clean directional longs.

Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CCL at 100 and NCLH at 97 form a cruise-line cluster at the top of the batch, both Very Strong with no pattern detail available, so the edge is purely vol-surface positioning. MARA at 99 adds a crypto-miner flavor, and BBY at 95 gives a consumer-discretionary read, making this an eclectic mix rather than a sector thesis. The score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 names at 80 or above, median at 75, which signals a genuinely active screen rather than a thin or padded one. NEUTRAL macro at 54.11 provides no directional tailwind, meaning the setup is vol-structural, not regime-driven, which is consistent with how VRP_MID is designed to work. First preference is CCL given the highest score and a liquid options chain, at moderate conviction pending confirmation of the vol surface setup intraday.

Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CR and ON both print at 100, the maximum possible score, Very Strong tier, with no pattern data provided to differentiate them further. LEVI at 85 is the third credible name, solidly Strong. CIFR at 62 stays in Watch territory and does not clear the high-conviction bar. The batch is top-heavy: 3 of 4 signals score 80 or above with a median of 85, a tight quality-skewed day rather than broad noisy participation. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.11), a non-event for this screener's contrarian hedging mechanism, so take CR and ON first at equal high conviction, with LEVI as a secondary add.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live 90-day tracking confirms the backtest edge, not contradicts it: 64.6% win rate and +4.1% average at 20 days beats SPY's -2.37% by 6.5 points across 494 resolved trades, the key number, though the monthly cohort trend warrants watching because May slipped to 56.5% WR and +1.7% avg after March's strong 67% WR and +4.9%, suggesting the edge is compressing as the year matures.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 500 closed · WR TB 43% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CMPR leads at a perfect 100 with a LAUNCH pattern, making it the first-look name today. JAZZ and PTON both print 95, also Very strong, also LAUNCH, and represent the next tier of interest. The score distribution is unusually compressed at the top: all 50 signals clear 80, median sits at 85, and nothing grades below the Strong threshold, which is a rare concentration that can reflect genuine tape quality or a narrow universe day. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.11, which does not add tailwind but does not block the setup either, so conviction stays moderate. Prioritize CMPR at the open with measured size; revisit JAZZ before adding PTON given the latter's history of whip-saw reversals.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Identifies vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag sell-vol setups at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$741.00 +1.65%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows WR 68% and avg +1.76%, beating SPY by just 0.05 points - negligible at the aggregate - but the Signal tier (n=7, WR 86%, avg +2.73%) carries the real edge, and May's cohort softening to +0.95% avg after April's exceptional +5.43% confirms this screener fires best in high-fear, high-dispersion regimes that the current NEUTRAL environment does not supply, which supports the KEEP verdict but argues for patience on tier selection.

🧠 Detects informed flow via options volume spikes over 5x the 20-day mean, filtered to calm and non-extended tickers to remove noise and late-chasing signals.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

2NLY ↑58 Watch$23.15 +0.87%BULLISH SWEEPCALL2216$1767K38.5049.4%0.57CONTRARIAN3x REPEAT4130.49
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

VIAV leads at score 76 (Signal tier) but carries an internal conflict: the pattern is a BEARISH SWEEP while the directional flag is LONG, which demands extra scrutiny before acting. AGNC at 68 (Signal tier) is cleaner, a straight BULLISH SWEEP with no directional ambiguity, making it the more straightforward candidate. The rest of the batch, IWM, KRE, NLY, SPY, GLD, and EWY, clusters between 55 and 61, all Watch-tier, with several more bearish-sweep-flagged-long contradictions that weaken conviction. The score distribution is compressed and bottom-heavy: 12 signals, median 56, zero names clearing 80, only 2 clearing 65, which is a thin and low-conviction batch by this screener's own standards. Macro is NEUTRAL (54.11), so there is no tailwind to lean on. First preference is AGNC at modest size given its clean signal alignment, with VIAV on hold until the sweep-direction conflict is resolved.

Construction

XLE and XLC are the only names worth acting on, both scoring 80 in the Strong tier and leading the batch by a clear margin. XLV at 65 (Signal tier) is the next step down, but pattern data is null across all eight picks, removing the one filter that would sharpen entry timing. The score distribution is top-heavy and thin: three names at 80 or above, then a hard drop to the median of 55, with five names stuck in Watch at 50-55. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.11 neither blocks nor catalyzes sector rotation, so the backdrop is permissive but not supportive. Lead with XLE at moderate conviction, given energy's tendency to show cleaner acceleration signals; XLC is equally scored but comms sector dynamics are noisier.

🧠 Spots deep-oversold equities printing capitulation volume plus a reversal candle, scoring mean-reversion probability via drawdown depth, ATR contraction, and volume spike.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape flatly contradicts the backtest: a 63.3% backtest win rate has decayed to 44.7% over 494 resolved trades, earning a KILL verdict, and while the screener technically beat SPY by 1.6 points (avg -0.8% vs SPY -2.4%), monthly cohorts show the improvement from a strong April (52.5% WR, +4.5% avg) has already stalled in May (46.1% WR, +2.4% avg), so there is no clean confirmation trend to lean on.

If taking one name, WLK at 100 gets first look, but size it at minimum conviction given the live verdict discussed below.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by annualized 90-day regression slope times R-squared, buying only trend-persistent names above MA100 when SPY clears its MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUM is the only name worth discussing - score 100, Very strong tier, standing alone at the top of a list where the median score is 12 and only one signal clears 65. PANW prints a 60 but lands in Watch, not a conviction entry. Everything else, CSCO, AMAT, UNH, CDNS, ELV, CAT, is scoring in the low double digits and belongs in the noise bin. The distribution is not clustered or broad - it is a single outlier dragging a weak tail, which is exactly the kind of batch this screener throws out in choppy tape. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.11), and a regime-gated momentum strategy at neutral is not the ideal setup. If you are going to act, HUM only, small size, and only if you can confirm SPY is above its MA200 today.

Two signals today, MU at 85 and CCL at 84, both scoring into the Strong tier with no pattern data available. The batch is about as thin as it gets, two names clustered tightly at 84-85 with zero score dispersion worth discussing. That matters because live tracking shows the Strong tier is the worst-performing bucket in this screener, five resolved trades at 50% win rate and -8.24% average 20-day return, so the tier label is actively misleading here. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.11 adds no tailwind. If forced to pick one, MU edges out on a one-point score advantage, but conviction is low given the tier's live track record, and sizing should reflect that.

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket correlations break above 2-sigma over 7 days, with severity scaled to the break count.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings price continuation where positive EPS surprise, gap persistence, and analyst revision momentum sustain drift over 1-3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$12.48 +9.47%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape across 495 resolved trades prints a 61.4% win rate and +4.4% average return against SPY at -0.03%, which confirms the live edge is real at the short horizon the backtest endorses (5 days, marginal), but the monthly cohort trend is a soft caution - May's 709-trade cohort ran 59.9% and +3.88% while April's six-trade cohort fell to 50% and +2.76%, the key number being WR compression as cohort size and recency diverge.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin at two names and should be treated accordingly. BBCP scores 90 (Very strong, PRIMED) and is the only name worth acting on - the score gap between it and BKE at 70 (Signal, BUY) is 20 points on a two-stock day, which means the distribution is not clustered but simply sparse. A median of 70 with one name above 80 and no names below 65 signals a weak day, not a broad opportunity set. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.11 is not a tailwind for PEAD plays, which perform best when risk appetite is directional. Take BBCP first at low-to-moderate conviction with reduced size, skip BKE until a stronger tape develops.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the KILL: overall WR of 49% and avg return of -1.2% are rescued only by the Very strong tier (54.3% WR, +0.77% avg), while Strong and Signal tiers both print negative averages, and the time-barred win rate of 28.8% is the key number that exposes how badly late-resolving trades are tracking.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is useless for ranking: all 50 signals print exactly 100, median 100, zero dispersion. Every name in the batch is tied, so score offers no differentiation today. Among the top picks, AMZN and APP are the most liquid names worth watching at the Very strong tier, while BA adds an aerospace cyclical angle. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (54.11), which neither supports nor undermines a momentum tilt. Given the KILL tracking verdict and the fact that only the Very strong tier produces positive average returns (+0.77%), any position should be sized at low conviction and confined to that tier. AMZN first, small.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking confirms a real equity edge overall - 59% win rate and +4.2% average versus SPY at -2.0% over 90 days - but the key number is that the Very strong tier averages -1.3% while the Watch tier averages +5.6%, a rank inversion that directly contradicts the intuitive read of score size and is reinforced by May cohort recovery to +4.0% following April's -0.73% average washout.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 35% · 24% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC leads at a perfect score of 100, joined by BEAM, CPB, and DFH all at 90, every one flagged PRIMED - but the live tier data is an immediate red flag: the Very strong cohort posts just a 45.8% win rate and an average loss of 1.3%, the worst tier in the screener by a wide margin. Score distribution is bottom-heavy, with all 50 signals clearing 65 but 35 of them clustering in the 65-79 band and only 15 reaching 80 or above, a thin high-conviction tail. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.1 provides no meaningful tailwind for a squeeze thesis that depends on vol and momentum alignment. First preference is ABX or AKR at 85/Strong, where the tracking cohort actually earns its keep, at low conviction given the regime and the tier inversion.

🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, surfacing names where volume leadership and price trend align simultaneously.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

2026-0521947%-2.1%+53.4%-85.6%
🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMAT scores 100 alongside a notable cable cluster in CHTR and CMCSA, both also at 100, suggesting sector-level rotation into telecom/cable rather than idiosyncratic stock moves. FBIN at 100 is the lone consumer name worth a second look given its separation from the cable theme. Score distribution is entirely compressed: median 96, all 50 signals clear the 80 threshold, which strips any rank differentiation and makes the scores nearly useless for ordering conviction. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.11), neither a tailwind nor a headwind. The critical problem is that every name today falls in the 'Very strong' tier, which is the worst-performing bucket in live tracking (43% WR, avg -3.2%), not the 'Strong' tier where the only positive alpha appears. Pass on the full batch; if forced, CHTR/CMCSA as a pair trade idea on sector rotation is the least-bad option, at low conviction.

🧠 Catches institutional options accumulation via large OTM block trades under 10 DTE on quiet names, confirmed by next-day open interest delta to filter closings.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tracking directly contradicts the screener thesis: 40% win rate and a -4.9% average 20-day return underperform SPY by 2.5 points across 469 resolved trades, earning a KILL verdict, yet the April and May 2026 monthly cohorts flip positive at +8.8% and +2.4% respectively, suggesting possible recent regime improvement that the 90d window is still dragging down. The key number is that -2.5 pt gap versus SPY on the full 90d sample - until that closes on a fresh rigorous backtest, the KILL verdict stands and any live exposure should be treated as observational only.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CWAN is the lone Very strong name at 93, the highest score in today's 33-signal batch and the only print that clears the tier ceiling. ARI at 84 and the SMQ/DGIN pair at 82 fill out the Strong tier, but all four lack pattern labels, so the conviction rests entirely on premium size and OI confirmation with no directional catalyst attached. The score distribution is moderately top-heavy: 6 names clear 80, median sits at 68, and 21 of 33 are at or above 65, meaning the batch is not thin but not exceptional either. A NEUTRAL macro at 54.11 neither supports nor blocks the setup. Critically, the 90d tier data shows Very strong and Strong names have averaged losses of -10.5% and -7.7% respectively over 20 days, far worse than Watch-tier names at -1.85%, so treat CWAN and ARI with lower conviction than the score implies. If forced to act, CWAN first on score alone, at minimal size until the tier-return inversion is resolved.

Construction

🧠 Detects mean-reversion entries where daily rejection-tail bars exceed 1.8x ATR at Dalton value-area extremes, the only configuration that backtested positive.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.66 -0.42%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW signals by skipping any setup where KVO or Force Index show conflicting volume momentum within a 3-day window, keeping only structurally clean flow.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑60 Watch$87.45 +0.10%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—94STRONG88
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is nearly empty: one signal, TLT at score 60, Watch tier, flagged as a BULLISH SWEEP on the long side. A score of 60 sits below both the 65 and 80 thresholds, meaning no name clears even the lower conviction bar. The distribution is not clustered or top-heavy, it is just thin. NEUTRAL macro at 54.11 does not actively oppose a TLT long - rates trades can work in directionless equity tape - but it provides no tailwind either. If forced to act, TLT is the only name available and low conviction is the honest read; size accordingly or wait for a second signal to confirm.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape posts a 47.8% win rate and +0.89% average return versus SPY at -0.88%, a thin 1.8-point edge that earns a WATCH verdict and contradicts the backtest Sharpe of 1.53, which itself carries a Bonferroni p-value of 1.0 and a CI lower bound of -0.34, meaning the backtest is statistically rejected; the one saving number is the Strong-tier live cohort at 57.97% win rate and +14.98% average, consistent with the April monthly cohort's +12.68%, but that tier is a small subset and does not rescue the overall signal.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEWR 48% · avg +1.1% ≈ SPY -0.8% (+1.9 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 500 resolved at +20d · WR 48% · avg +1.1% · vs SPY -0.8%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 520 closed · WR TB 47% · 8% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. AGL is the only name worth touching, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier with no pattern label but clear breakout strength separation from the rest of the batch. Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day KEEP verdict (55% WR, avg +0.2%, +2.0pts vs SPY) looks acceptable in aggregate, but the tier breakdown exposes the core problem: Strong and Very strong names - exactly today's top candidates - have lost 9.7% and 3.3% on average as shorts, the March-2026 cohort responsible for the best monthly result (68.5% WR, avg +6.5%) is the same cluster the REJECTED backtest flagged as a p=0.084 artefact, and the headline numbers are carried almost entirely by the 409 Watch-tier names at +0.58%, confirming the signal breaks down precisely where conviction is highest.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NVCT leads today at score 90 with a BREAKDOWN pattern, the sole Very strong signal, but that tier has gone 0-for-1 historically with an avg short loss of 3.3%. DFTX at 85 and QURE at 80 are both Strong-tier TRIGGER patterns, and Strong has delivered a 28.6% win rate with an avg short loss of 9.7% over the last 90 days, meaning the highest-conviction names have reliably squeezed rather than collapsed. All 50 signals score at least 65 with the median at 70 and only 3 names at 80 or above, so the batch is compressed rather than top-heavy, but the weight sits exactly in the tiers with the worst live track record. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54) provides no directional tailwind for shorts. Prefer none of the three highest-scored names; if forced to act, the Signal-tier names at 75 - APPS, BLZE, BTQ, GFS, NEO - sit in the tier that at least wins more than half the time, and only at minimum size.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every single name in the top 50 scores 99 or 100, median 99, all 50 clearing the 80-tier threshold, which means the model is producing zero rank discrimination today - this is a score ceiling problem, not a high-conviction signal. The batch is a near-pure semiconductor and storage sweep: STX and WDC dominate storage hardware, LRCX and AMAT anchor wafer equipment, AMD and INTC represent logic, DELL and FLEX add systems and EMS exposure. That sector concentration against a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.11) reads as a factor crowding event, not genuine alpha generation. No patterns are available for any name to add color. Given a REJECTED backtest and fully compressed scores, conviction is low across the board; if forced to pick one, LRCX gets first look on equipment cycle leverage, but size should be negligible until scores show real dispersion.

🧠 Flags stocks with weekly RSI(14) below 30 then daily crossover and candlestick confirmation to capture short-term mean-reversion bounces.

Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape confirms the KILL verdict and contradicts the screener: the 90-day cohort averages +2.47% against SPY +4.27%, a -1.8 point lag, and the monthly cohort trend is deteriorating sharply, with March at +6.19%, April at +5.27%, and May collapsing to -0.37%.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and OI delta in crypto-linked ETFs, weighting flow intensity against spot crypto correlation to flag momentum shifts.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The headline KEEP verdict (64% WR, +1.3% avg, +1.5 pts vs SPY) is entirely a Watch-tier artifact carrying 66 of 75 resolved trades, while Signal and Strong tiers have delivered -20.2% and -13.2% avg respectively - which directly contradicts leaning on today's highest-scored names, and the rejected backtest (0/16 signals surviving adversarial validation post-2024 spot-ETF launch) reinforces that the edge here is thin and tier-inverted.

CLSK leads at score 80 (Strong, TRIGGER) and CORZ follows at 75 (Signal, BUY), but tier-level tracking data inverts the expected hierarchy - Strong produced a -13.2% avg on its one resolved trade and Signal ran -20.2% avg across 8 trades, while the Watch tier where HUT, MARA, and RIOT sit is where the realized edge actually lives at +4.1% avg and 71% win rate. Five signals total, one at or above 80, two at or above 65 - a thin and top-heavy batch with most weight clustered in the Watch band. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 54.11, providing no directional assist for a crypto-flow screen. If forced to act, HUT at score 60 is the least bad entry given the historical tier distribution, at low conviction only.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

IFRA is the only name worth leading with, scoring 81 and the sole Strong-tier signal in a 50-name batch where just 1 ticker clears 80 and 19 clear 65. The infrastructure complex dominates the top of the list, with NFRA at 72 and PAVE at 71 echoing the same thesis, plus PHO and FIW clustering around 69 on the water infrastructure angle. ARKG at 76 is the lone outlier in a different direction, flagging genomics momentum, but without a pattern tag its edge is thin. The score distribution is top-heavy and concentrated rather than broad, with the median sitting at 62 and the mass of the batch bunched in the 60s. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54 neither validates nor kills an infrastructure tilt, but it does not argue for chasing. Preference is IFRA first at low-to-moderate conviction, with ARKG as a speculative secondary only if the genomics setup develops further detail.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers print score 100 today - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM - all "Very strong" tier, all LONG, all missing pattern data, which limits conviction immediately. The presence of SRTY, an inverse small-cap ETF, sitting alongside growth names like CELH and BL at identical scores is an unusual mix that either reflects genuine sector-rotation pressure in small caps or a screener artifact worth flagging before acting. Score distribution is top-heavy: 41 of 50 signals (82%) clear 65, with 12 at 80 or above, so the batch is not thin but it is clustered near the ceiling. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.11), offering no directional help. Take CELH first on a modest position - beaten-down consumer name, maximum score, but hold conviction at low-to-moderate given missing pattern context and a flat macro read.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names clearing the Very Strong tier, meaning multiple funds filed new or increased positions in the same quarter, with a score gap wide enough to separate them from the field. AVGO at 79 is the next name down, sitting just below the 80 threshold with a Signal-tier read. Score distribution is top-heavy and thin at the top: only 2 of 50 signals reach 80+, 13 reach 65+, and the median sits at 55, meaning most of this batch carries low-to-middling conviction. Macro is NEUTRAL at 54.11, which neither supports nor blocks a long bias, but the structural 60-to-90-day staleness in 13F data means entry prices have already repriced since these filings were made. ASML is the first name to work given its score dominance, but size it for the lag risk baked into the source data.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and JEF both print 100 and are the first names to examine, though pattern fields are null across the board so the basis for differentiation is pure rank-times-dollar construction. PBLS, KARD, and CGON follow at 99 with no additional context. The score distribution is effectively useless as a filter: all 47 signals cluster between 93 (median) and 100 (max), every signal clears the 80 threshold, and the screener is running at ceiling. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.11 is not a headwind but offers no amplification for insider-driven ideas. Given the ceiling compression, treat SVRE and JEF as the first-look names at low-to-moderate conviction, and wait for fundamental context before sizing.

🧠 Ranks US equities by Tobias Carlisle's EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing the cheapest operating-earnings bargains; score 100 is the day's deepest value name.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).
Top picks: $BZ $WRD $KROS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayAcquirer's MultipleEVOperating EarningsMarket CapEV source

15USNA ↑53 Watch$21.01 +1.50%4.07$0.27B$0.07B$0.35Byfinance
16CRMD ↑50 Watch$7.64 -2.68%4.32$0.65B$0.15B$0.66Byfinance
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Zero of 37 tracked positions have resolved and the backtest has not been run, so there is no live win rate, no avg vs SPY, and no monthly cohort trend to confirm or contradict anything - the only number is 0.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ at score 100 and WRD at 97 lead the batch, both Very strong tier, making them the primary candidates on pure cheapness rank. KROS at 93 rounds out the top three, also Very strong. The score distribution is top-heavy rather than thin: 7 of 16 signals clear 80 and 11 clear 65 against a median of 73, so today's batch has genuine spread at the top. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 54.11, which is indifferent territory for deep value, neither a tailwind nor a headwind. No pattern data exists for any name, the backtest has not been run, and zero trades have resolved, so conviction must be kept low across the board. BZ first if you must touch anything, but size it accordingly.

🧠 Flags retail euphoria via social attention z-score, call/put skew, and parabolic price stretch to identify short-side mean-reversion candidates.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $CAG $BKNG $ARE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg

12BE ↓53 Watch$275.01 +9.12%GREED0.538.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The formal backtest is absent (N=17, below the 30-trade threshold), so the 90-day live tape is the only statistical reference: WR 61%, avg +0.66% vs SPY -0.40%, a +1.1-point edge that earns a KEEP, but May 2026 (WR 64%, avg +1.79%) and April 2026 (WR 17%, avg -15.89%, n=6) show sharp regime sensitivity, and the Signal tier - where most of today's picks land - averages -7.43% across 15 resolved trades, the single most important number cutting against today's batch.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CAG at 100 and BKNG at 97 are the only Very strong reads and are the names to focus on, though both carry null pattern fields, which thin the entry-timing case. The remaining five Signal-tier picks (ARE, BXP, B, ANET, ASTS, scores 68-73) sit in the weakest tier by 90-day average return, so their inclusion drags the batch quality. Score distribution is narrow and bottom-heavy: 12 total signals, median 68, just 2 clearing 80. Macro NEUTRAL at 54.11 does not amplify the short thesis but does not block it. Lead with CAG at moderate conviction, hold BKNG as the second look, and treat the Signal-tier cluster as watch-list only until pattern data appears.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider accumulation via SEC Form 4 cluster buys, weighting C-suite rank and total dollar commitment to front-run informed demand.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $ADSK $BRID $FCBM
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value
1ADSK ↑60 Watch$195.24 -0.52%3✓✓$457K

11GOTU ↑50 Watch$1.73 -0.57%3✓·$243K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener earns its keep, with a 59% win rate and a +4.0% avg 20-day return versus SPY +0.6%, and the May 2026 cohort at 63% win rate and +4.7% avg shows the edge is strengthening, not decaying, though the absence of any formal backtest means those numbers carry no Sharpe or Bonferroni anchor yet.

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging within already-moving LATE_CHASE or RIDING sectors, scoring each by sector relative strength multiplied by the stock's underperformance gap.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 Identifies top relative-strength tickers within leading sectors using 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster ranking.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.84 -1.35%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$71.17 -1.43%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$8.12 +10.03%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$132.83 -2.34%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$90.35 -0.71%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects failed breakdowns below VAL or breakouts above VAH that reverse intraday, trapping positioned traders and creating mean-reversion entries.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ABOS $AGEN $APPN
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ABOS ↑75● Signal$2.77 +17.37%BEAR_TRAP2.162.600.15
2AGEN ↑70● Signal$3.15 +3.96%BEAR_TRAP2.813.420.23
3APPN ↑68● Signal$22.48 +2.32%BEAR_TRAP20.4921.831.44
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape looks encouraging on the surface - 76.9% win rate and +8.0% avg return over 20 days against SPY at -0.86% across 13 resolved trades - but with only one monthly cohort and 13 resolved observations the system is formally on WATCH and the sample is too thin to call the edge confirmed or to compare against a backtest that does not yet exist.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags 25-delta put/call IV skew spikes vs 90-day baseline as contrarian long signals when price stays calm despite rising hedging demand.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $TSLA $COST
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1TSLA ↓100●●● Very strong$411.84 +8.46%159%94—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2COST ↓50 Watch$946.68 -0.62%66%25—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports keeping this screener - 55% win rate, +9.9% avg, +8.5pts above SPY - and May and April cohorts hold steady at +10.1% and +9.2%, but the critical anomaly is a tier inversion where Watch names (69% WR, +16.9% avg) dramatically outperform Very Strong picks (17% WR, -5.1% avg), meaning higher skew intensity is a fade, not confirmation, and today's lower-scored Watch names actually carry the better historical return profile.

🧠 Flags stocks where put implied volatility overwhelms call IV at extreme percentiles, signaling unusual institutional hedging or directional put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$372.45 +2.04%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices the Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, targeting long vol before realization.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1145.28 +1.14%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is one name: MU at score 50, Watch tier, SHORT direction, no pattern populated. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 z-score exceeds 1.5 sigma over 90 days, surfacing inflated fear premiums as short-vol candidates.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓61 Watch$73.78 -0.04%46%30%1.53FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.

🧠 The 90-day tape posts WR 46% and avg +2.4% versus SPY +1.4%, a slim positive read, but May 2026 collapsed to -0.28% average from April's +10.8%, and the Strong tier's -3.87% average actively undercuts tier ordering, so the recent monthly trend contradicts any building confidence in the screener, with May's -0.28% as the number that matters.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bull traps where price breaks above the Value Area High then closes back inside, signaling trapped longs as the short entry mechanism.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AMKR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AMKR ↓60 Watch$82.34 +4.60%BULL_TRAP83.4470.36
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Nine resolved trades out of 67 is too thin to confirm or contradict anything, but the key number is the Watch tier's 16.7% win rate and -9.96% average 20-day return, which is where today's only signal sits - the one tier that is actively losing money is exactly where AMKR lands.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (9 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 67 signals captured · 9 resolved at +20d · WR 33% · avg -0.7% · vs SPY -1.8%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 37 closed · WR TB 41% · 5% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.
Does the strength predict? (resolved signals by tier, +20d)

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=2 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and Kerberos-style options flow to surface mean-reversion or directional vol setups.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$22.55 -4.29%58%761%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape is a hard contradiction of any implied edge: WR 24%, avg return -9.7% vs SPY -0.5%, a -9.2 point gap, and the May 2026 cohort (n=24) replicates that damage with WR 25% and avg -9.1%, giving the screener a KILL verdict before a formal backtest even exists.

Altman

STARTER · untested
no backtest stats yet

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Altman Z-score financial distress logic to surface either turnaround or short candidates based on credit health deterioration or recovery.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

🧠 STFR screens for a specific signal pattern via proprietary EOD logic; exact construction requires review of the source script.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA detects institutional footprints via volume-spread divergence, flagging accumulation or distribution where price range and volume conflict with trend.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
